title: Friday Sermon 26 January 1990
description:
When she came it was said ‘Is thy throne like this?’  She replied ‘It is as though it were the  same.  We had been given knowledge before your vast resources and we have already believed.’

So he prevented her from worshipping that which she used to worship beside Allah; for  she came of a disbelieving nation.  

It was said to her ‘Enter the palace.’  When she saw it she thought it to be a great expanse of water and she uncovered her shanks.  He said ‘It is a hall paved smooth with slabs of glass.’  She said ‘My Lord!  Indeed I wronged my soul and I submit myself with Solomon to Allah the Lord of the worlds.’

('The Holy Quran'.  Al Naml [The Ant]: 43 - 45).

The Prophet Solomon preached asking her to become a Muslim, while she had already become a Muslim.  She had submitted.  Now she was asked to believe in the truth offered by Solomon.  One can be a Muslim without being a believer.  We also read in The Holy Quran: 

The Arabs of the desert say ‘We believe.’  Say ‘You not that you have believed but rather say:  We have accepted Islam; for the faith has not yet entered into your hearts.’  But if you obey  Allah and His Messenger He will not detract anything from your deeds.  Verily Allah is Most  Forgiving, Merciful.

(Al Hujarat [The Inner Chambers]: 15).

On the other hand, the Prophet Abraham (peace be on him), was asked to submit, while he already had a strong belief in Allah.  Islam is a necessity after one's having believed.  One's submission is a testimony of one's faith.  There can be a submission without having faith, but no faith without submission.  So the real Islam is the outcome of a real faith.  From The Holy Quran and the Hadith we know that faith increases and decreases.  With the increase of faith, spiritual insight also increases and with this Islam (submission in practice) also increases.  An Ahmadi would know that submission before the authorities is mandatory.  This is not a calling to blind faith.  It is something that follows a real faith, which is a result of spiritual insight and not a belief without thinking, pondering and reasoning.

It is the sign of true faith that it increases with thinking and reasoning and the brightening of one's intellect.  Such as in the examples of Hazrat Chaudhry Zafarullah Khan and Dr. Abdus Salam.  I harbour deep sorrow for those who instead of submitting before their Emirs, form groups against them.


The text http://www.alislam.org/urdu/sermon/FST19900126-UR.pdf
